prompt.text: Simplify the compound expression to one power of z. Assume z is non-zero.

prompt.spoken_text: open parenthesis z to the power of 0 times z to the power of minus 5 close parenthesis times open parenthesis z to the power of 3 close parenthesis to the power of 3 times z to the power of 15 divided by z to the power of 3

hints.text: Account for the zero and negative exponents before combining every z power.
Multiply nested exponents, then add numerator exponents and subtract denominator exponents.
A correct completed form is z^16.

prompt.text: Simplify the compound expression to one power of z. Assume z is non-zero.

prompt.spoken_text: open parenthesis z to the power of 0 times z to the power of minus 7 close parenthesis times open parenthesis z to the power of 2 close parenthesis to the power of 5 times z to the power of 20 divided by z to the power of 6

hints.text: Account for the zero and negative exponents before combining every z power.
Multiply nested exponents, then add numerator exponents and subtract denominator exponents.
A correct completed form is z^17.

prompt.text: Simplify the compound expression to one power of z. Assume z is non-zero.

prompt.spoken_text: open parenthesis z to the power of 0 times z to the power of minus 2 close parenthesis times open parenthesis z to the power of 2 close parenthesis to the power of 3 times z to the power of 10 divided by z to the power of 1

hints.text: Account for the zero and negative exponents before combining every z power.
Multiply nested exponents, then add numerator exponents and subtract denominator exponents.

prompt.text: Simplify the expression using the quotient rule for equal bases. Assume z is non-zero.

prompt.spoken_text: z to the power of 3 divided by z to the power of 9

hints.text: Keep the common base and combine the exponents.
A quotient of equal bases calls for subtracting the denominator exponent.
A correct completed form is z^-6.

prompt.text: Simplify the power of a power.

prompt.spoken_text: open parenthesis z to the power of 3 close parenthesis to the power of 4

hints.text: Keep the base while combining the nested exponents.
A power raised to a power calls for multiplying the exponents.
A correct completed form is z^12.

prompt.text: Rewrite the expression using only positive exponents. Assume z is non-zero.

prompt.spoken_text: z to the power of minus 9

hints.text: A negative exponent moves the powered factor across the fraction bar.
Write the powered base in the denominator and make its exponent positive.
A correct completed form is 1/z^9.

prompt.text: Simplify using the zero-exponent rule. Assume z is non-zero.

prompt.spoken_text: z to the power of 0

hints.text: Use the multiplicative-identity result of the zero-exponent rule.
The ratio interpretation of a zero exponent has equal numerator and denominator.
A correct completed form is 1.

prompt.text: Simplify the expression using the product rule for equal bases.

prompt.spoken_text: z to the power of 2 times z to the power of 3

hints.text: Keep the common base and combine the exponents.
A product of equal bases calls for adding their exponents.
A correct completed form is z^5.

prompt.text: Simplify the expression using the quotient rule for equal bases. Assume z is non-zero.

prompt.spoken_text: z to the power of 11 divided by z to the power of 4

hints.text: Keep the common base and combine the exponents.

A correct completed form is z^7.

prompt.spoken_text: h open parenthesis x close parenthesis equals f open parenthesis x close parenthesis divided by g open parenthesis x close parenthesis

g open parenthesis 6 close parenthesis equals 7
f prime open parenthesis 6 close parenthesis equals 4
g prime open parenthesis 6 close parenthesis equals 5

prompt.text: Differentiate the polynomial with respect to x.

prompt.spoken_text: 3 times x to the power of 12 plus 4 times x to the power of 9 minus 6 times x to the power of 5 plus 2

Apply the constant-multiple and power rules to each nonconstant term.
A correct completed form is 36*x^11+36*x^8-30*x^4.
